title: Support subitems in time tracking widget
excerpt: The calculation currently only sums columns from the main tasks. Since timetracking column is also available on subtasks, it should ideally sum these as well.

posts.author: locomplit (Lars Ørnlo)
posts.cookedHtml: The calculation currently only sums columns from the main tasks. Since timetracking column is also available on subtasks, it should ideally sum these as well.

posts.author: LucasTavares (Lucas Tavares)
posts.cookedHtml: Having the same problem and havent find a anwer yet. I really need the subtask functionallity and the dashboard not showing the time in this type of task is killing me.

posts.author: simonwind (Simon wind )
posts.cookedHtml: Having the same problem here. Any ideas how to solve this or any update planned Monday.com??

posts.author: bmontoyataylor (Brittany Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, I’ve put together a workaround in my board for this problem and it seems to have done the trick. Here it is:

  1. In the subitems, open the time tracking column settings and select “Show Summary on Parent Item”. I renamed the column ‘Subitem Time Tracking’.

  2. For the time tracking column on the parent menu, I renamed it ‘Parent Item Time Tracking’.

  3. Now add a Formula column. I renamed it ‘Time Totals’. Enter this formula to add the two columns together and equate to total hours:

SUM({Subitem Time Tracking},{Parent Item Time Tracking})/60/60

  1. Now SUM will appear in the bottom right hand corner of the item. Click on SUM and you can add your own UNIT title - I added Hours. So now at the bottom of the task I can see the total hours, including subitem hours!

Screen Shot 2020-08-18 at 9.40.38 PM2701×690 87.4 KB

posts.author: JaredFoxworthy (Jared)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump.

Any update here? This really is basic stuff. Why bother letting us time track at sub-task level if we can’t target Subtask time tracked (or the rollup column from pulse-level?)

How can any company track actual vs. allocated time using Monday.com and subtasks?

This is a significant product gap. Cmon guys! Such a good product. Fix this and rule the roost - because Asana, ClickUp, etc are hot on your tails.

posts.author: JaredFoxworthy (Jared)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bumping my bump. Solved this with the same hack as bmontoyataylor above.

My specific use case: “Pull rollup Subtask Time Tracked into a MDC dashboard” since my company is running Pulse > Sub pulse structure.

As we know, we can’t use the Time Tracking dashboard widget and hook into subtask time tracked (which made the whole concept of subtask time tracking a moot point since there was no way to accurately report on it).

Two screenshots attached showing the Board > Dashboard widget relationship.

Using the Formula column at board level to pull in the Subtask Time Tracked rollup. This gives a weird number which is a conversion of the Time Track rollup - but in seconds.

Simply modify the formula to divide by 60 and voila.

dashboard reporting subtask effort725×433 6.7 KB

Board level with subtask rollup actualized1524×224 21.3 KB

posts.author: SRM_Travis (Travis Salyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is quickly becoming a dealbreaker for continuing to use this service…

What is the purpose of tracking time if you can’t track subtasks? Subtasks that you already have timers and tracking built into?

This is BASIC reporting functionality that people are requesting. Please consider fixing this.

posts.author: cavegnman (Tony Cavegn)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to add another vote for this functionality

posts.author: bperdue (Bryan Perdue)
posts.cookedHtml: Not having this feature is most likely the most frustrating thing about Monday. My two most desired features is being able to do time tracking on the sub items and report on it accurately.

posts.author: Aliado (Casey Shagena)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is also becoming a deal breaker for us too. Since you cannot have employees track time on the same task simultaneously, and you ALSO don’t have a notes feature, we need a way for them to track on the same project at the same time and add a note so we know what they are working on. So we use Subtasks. Why have the feature to add a Subtask time tracking if we don’t have reporting on it?
Yes, we can see how much time was tracked with the rollup feature, but it doesn’t show the time employees clock over a period of time and doesn’t account for the project and time frame in a reporting dashboard (which is what we use for client billing). PLEASE fix this ASAP!

posts.author: Oink (Felix Trash)
posts.cookedHtml: Bump. This makes monday.com adding time tracking to subitems essentially redundant. The tracked hours feature should be available from timetracked subitems.

posts.author: TimothyReynal (Tim)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree with all of the above…subtask time tracking without rolling up to the parent task is a headache. Also while at it, allow an option to forecast time in the future (for companies who have to submit timecards on Friday morning for a payroll period that ends on Saturday. This is common for consulting companies for timely client billing.)

posts.author: Patnard (Patrick Chenard)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a feature that is a must right now as there is no easy way to work around the lack of this feature

posts.author: Henryj21 (Henry)
posts.cookedHtml: just commenting so hopefully i am notified when this comes out. I am also very interested in this. I assume if people keep asking. eventually we will get it.

posts.author: solarr (Solar)
posts.cookedHtml:
New Monday user here. There are few issues with sub items:

  • Can’t hide columns from views that we want to share with clients so we have to be careful on what we log in the columns or rely on logging only in the parent item
  • Time tracking widget does not calculate logged time in subitems

Big general time logging issue:

  • For example, if we have a team meeting - not everyone can log time for the same time for example 10:00am - 11:00am. If there’s 5 of us we have log different timeframes or have ridiculous amounts of sub tasks but it does not calculate the total time logged unless you use the roll up column on the parent items and use a hack as seen in this thread.

While we wait for Monday to fix these major issues, does anyone use decent apps that doesn’t cost a fortune?

posts.author: Nikki (Nikki Clemmons)
posts.cookedHtml: I have used this and really love it, was starting to use time tracking in Monday and found this same issue. TrackingTime MS Teams app on a Team. I created a Time Tracking Team and under General added the app. Works amazing. But I really wish Monday would add the rollup so it’s all in one place.

posts.author: arkadidp (Arkadi De Proft)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe this is a features that needs to be requested and voted on and that hasn’t been implemented since it’s been requested years ago. It makes tracking time in sub-items entirely useless.

posts.author: agilel (Liz Ferrusca)
posts.cookedHtml: I was doing this, but it won’t separate the parent item time tracking by the person that actually time tracked it. It is not an efficient way to understand my teams workload, if I can’t see each individuals time tracking.

posts.author: agilel (Liz Ferrusca)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this feature? I don’t understand how there is a sub-item time tracking function if it is not going to work with the Time tracking widget.

posts.author: roee (sade)
posts.cookedHtml: Im joining the question, any updates?

posts.author: DerekErb (Derek Erb)
posts.cookedHtml:
When we add time tracking information in a sub-item, in a time tracking column, the time spent does not get added to the total time spent on the parent task and therefore does not get added to the total time spent on the item.

The time spent/tracked gets lost from the overall calculation and presentation.

We have had to remove the time tracking column from sub-items so our people don’t use them as that information is lost for the project.

Time-tracking information for sub-items should be added to, and included in, the overall time spent for the parent task and the parent project.

posts.author: marcbrown (Marc Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: This is exactly what we are wanting to do! This would be huge for us. Please add this capability Monday!

posts.author: Londya (Londya)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m so grateful for this community! Two times already I’ve found solutions to my problems! Thank you! This was jsut what I’ve been trying to figure out for the last few hours. SO simple! smh lol

posts.author: emcarr (Emilie Carr)
posts.cookedHtml: @DerekErb I totally agree with this! This is also the major issue for us. Initially I thought I could try a similar workaround as @bmontoyataylor suggested, but the problem is that we have several dashboard reports that we need for invoicing, and they don’t recognize the new formula column as a time. SO frustrating. I really wish that, when you added a subitem time tracking column, there was a popup that asked you if you wanted the time to roll up into the parent column, or it was something you could toggle on and off at the board or account level.

posts.author: kimmikaze (Kim Wormer)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be an awesome feature. We have soooo many tasks during a project and we have to group them manually like 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2, 1.2.1 and so on. Our boss needs the time tracked seperately for billing, but this is very confusing for the team members because it generates hundreds of tasks. If sub-tasks were trackable and all would summarized to the main task, everything would be so much easier.

posts.cookedHtml: This topic needs to be bumped - if multiple users cannot simultaneously track time against the same project, then sub-items need to be used. But there absolutely needs to be a function for this time to roll up to the parent in a way that is accessible as time for widgets / dashboards.

posts.author: Brandon (Brandon Dole)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does anyone know of any progress on this?
We too have been hugely struggling with Monday’s subitem time tracking and it’s always one issue after another with workarounds.

Problem Subitem time isn’t added to parent item’s time tracking (which should be a checkbox)?
Workaround: Create the subitem total column
New Problem: Dashboard Widgets can’t look at this column.
Workaround: Try export in an effort to use an external solution?
New Problem: Monday doesn’t export itemized Subitem time entries.

Time Tracking is just a never-ending challenge for an otherwise great platform.

posts.author: AdamCPU (Adam Raphael)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, all this! In addition it makes charts etc very hard/impossible to create for time tracking as you end up using different column types.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Time tracking dashboard should calculate from subtasks as well - #25 by TimothyReynal - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

Thanks!

posts.author: rgobatto (rgobatto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

A solution that worked for me (not ideal, but ok), is to use a graphic, with stacked bar and expanded view, in the dashboard (1st image). The graphic counts the time tracking of each subitem and in the configs I chose to show by person per mounth.

This works for project board (2nd image). Our company is small, few people per project.

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I mentioned it in another time tracking based thread, so I’m not going to go too extensively into it here (just link over there - Improvements on Time tracking column - #184 by EMorton), but the overall gist is that another solution for this is instead of the time tracking widget, use the workload widget and some creative column use to indicate how many hours someone has worked each day/week/month. Can allow for setting a ‘capacity’ to indicate when someone’s hitting their total hours for the week, as well as be filtered as well by any number of fields/columns that you add to the items or subitems.
For example, here’s a snapshot of what it looks like with a selection of hours against a few filtered tasks from the last couple of weeks.

image760×323 16.4 KB

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community,

Thank you for this valuable feedback. At the moment, time tracking is not in our upcoming roadmap since we are focusing on other areas of the platform. While we do not currently support time tracking, there are some great apps in the monday marketplace to help with your needs. I recomendd checking out Trackets by Avisi Apps or 7pace by Appfire.

While this is not on the roadmap at moment, I will let you know if things change in the future.

Keep sharing your ideas and voting on the features most important for you. To learn more about our new feature request process check out this post. All you need to know about our new Feature Request process

posts.author: ccrouse (Charles)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe that this ticket has been open since May of 2020 and has so many up votes but Monday.com continues to ignore this fundamental piece of functionality.

posts.author: christinerivera718 (chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
No sure if this will help

but it worked for me

Parent & subitem tracking

SUM({Time Tracking}+{Subitems Time Tracking})/60/60

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: this only works, however, if only one person ever tracks time in one subitem since your “person” filter does not look at the person who created the time entry but the column.

posts.author: darrylclark (Darryl)
posts.cookedHtml:
I find this really frustrating and it may well become a reason to move away from Monday (amongst many other time tracking challenges in the platform - multiple people tracking at the same time/holidays/profiles).

Maybe I’m cynical but whilst Monday.com are making money by recommending time tracking apps from their market place they aren’t going to work on their own version. I’ve got over 100 users so going to my bosses to ask for another $5/month/user for >100 users to fix a piece of software is not going to go down well.

posts.author: AndrewPike.Fogo (Andrew Pike)
posts.cookedHtml: This is super frustrating. It is really limiting the usefulness of insights from my dashboards in Monday. I would love to see some dev effort here to add in support for sub-item time tracking in dashboard widgets.

posts.author: arun.paudyal (Arun Paudyal [Decadis AG])
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear monday Community,

I’m Arun from Decadis AG. We’ve listened closely to your feedback, and we understand the challenges you’ve been facing. That’s why we’ve worked hard to deliver a solution that I believe will meet your expectations.

Our app, sumUp for monday, is specifically designed to simplify use cases like yours. It includes two robust widgets—Group By and Two Dimensional—to make pivoting and summation effortless across one or two dimensions.

In our latest release, we’ve taken it a step further by introducing pivoting for sub-items and adding support for the time tracking column, making the app even more versatile and powerful. The best part? you can add as many widgets as you need and easily switch between item and sub-item modes.

Check out the screencast below—I’m confident you’ll love what you see!

(For fullscreen view: Right click on the gif → and select ‘Open Image in New Tab’)

Give it a try!

If you have any questions or would like to explore how sumUp can address your specific needs, please don’t hesitate to reach out. I’m here to help and would love to hear from you!

CC: @Julietteb

Best regards,
Arun

posts.author: spelkey (Shannon Pelkey)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb What is the best way to get this on the roadmap?

posts.author: rossg (Ross G)
posts.cookedHtml:
May 2020. Has anyone ever seen a thread this long unresolved?

A workflow management platform, famously used for time tracking, with a default time tracking feature for subitems, doesn’t allow anyone to see who has tracked time on subitems. Incredible

posts.author: TDGregory (Tabetha)
posts.cookedHtml: I would really like to see this added to the roadmap. I don’t like that we have to purchase a different solution to resolve this issue. In my mind that is not a resolution, that is a workaround.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,

Throwing in another solution here, since I believe it can be still useful for anyone stumbling across this topic.
The Board Email Reports app includes a Time Tracking report that works even on the subitem level.
You can set it up with a simple automation rule — it will send you an XLS file on a recurring basis, showing how long each subitem has been tracked over your selected time period.

Here’s what it looks like:

image1374×500 61 KB

image983×566 39 KB

image1517×734 107 KB

We can customize the reports for your use case, just share it with our team.

Hope this helps someone looking for a way to report on time tracking in subitems!

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do that with the Analytics & Reports add-on by Screenful which allows reporting time tracking data both on top level items and subitems, and filtering the charts based on who logged the time. Charts can be placed on Monday dashboard and automatically emailed on set schedule. Here’s an example report:

CleanShot 2025-07-23 at 20.07.521559×864 107 KB

You can learn more from this guide: Creating charts based on time tracking data - Screenful Guide for monday.com

posts.author: LexiNiccolls (Lexi )
posts.cookedHtml:
5 and a half years later, and this isn’t even on the roadmap. I’m quite shocked in all honesty, and I’ll be turning to other work management tools in future for my clients.

I’ve been having the biggest headache with Monday these past few months over (in my opinion) fundamental and basic functionalities for managing work. What seemed to be such a promising tool back when I started using it in 2020, it now feels severely lacking and behind the curve. So disappointed.
